about.body1: Biraj Dental Pvt. Ltd. has been serving the Bhaktapur community with dedication and care. What started as a single clinic has grown into one of Bagmati Province's most trusted dental care networks, now operating 9 locations across the region.
about.body2: Founded by Dr. Bodh Raj Mainali (NMC No. 21549), our team of experienced dental professionals combines modern techniques with genuine compassion. We believe that everyone deserves access to quality dental care — and we've made that our mission since day one.
about.body3: With our main branch at Gathaghar Chowk, Bhaktapur and 8 additional locations across Bagmati Province, we ensure expert dental care is never far from home.

posts.title: 5 Signs You Need a Dental Checkup Right Away
posts.excerpt: Don't wait for pain to visit the dentist. These warning signs mean it's time to book an appointment.
posts.author: Dr. Bodh Raj Mainali

content.text: Many people assume that if their teeth don't hurt, everything is fine. But dental problems often develop silently — without pain or obvious symptoms — until they reach an advanced stage. At Biraj Dental in Bhaktapur, we see patients every week who waited too long because they had no warning signs. Here are five indicators that you should book a checkup immediately.
1. Bleeding Gums When Brushing or Flossing

Healthy gums should not bleed during normal brushing or flossing. If you notice pink on your toothbrush or in the sink, it is often the earliest sign of gingivitis — the first stage of gum disease. Left untreated, gingivitis can progress to periodontitis, which damages the bone supporting your teeth and can lead to tooth loss. The good news is that early-stage gum disease is completely reversible with a professional cleaning and improved home care.
2. Persistent Bad Breath

Occasional bad breath after eating garlic or coffee is normal. But chronic halitosis that persists despite good oral hygiene often signals an underlying problem. It could be caused by bacteria buildup below the gum line, an untreated cavity, dry mouth, or even a sinus infection. A dental examination can identify the root cause and recommend the right treatment.
3. Tooth Sensitivity to Hot, Cold, or Sweet

A brief twinge when eating ice cream is common. But sharp, lingering pain from temperature changes or sugary foods often indicates enamel erosion, a cracked tooth, or an exposed root. At our Biraj Dental clinic in Bhaktapur, we regularly treat sensitivity with fluoride varnishes, desensitizing toothpaste recommendations, or restorative fillings depending on the cause.
4. Visible Changes in Your Teeth or Gums

Look for dark spots on teeth, white patches on your gums, receding gum lines, or any swelling. These visual changes can indicate decay, infection, or oral health conditions that need professional attention. Even a small chip or crack should be evaluated — what seems minor can quickly worsen.
5. It Has Been More Than Six Months Since Your Last Visit

The simplest sign of all: if you cannot remember when you last saw a dentist, it is time. The Nepali Dental Council and international dental associations both recommend checkups every six months for most adults. Regular visits catch problems early when they are smaller, simpler, and far less expensive to treat.

At Biraj Dental Pvt. Ltd. in Bhaktapur, we offer comprehensive checkups starting from just NPR 800. Our experienced team uses modern diagnostic tools to give you a complete picture of your oral health. Call us at 9851031257 or book online — your future self will thank you.

posts.title: Root Canal Treatment: What to Expect
posts.excerpt: Myths about root canals keep people from getting needed treatment. Here's the truth about the procedure.
posts.author: Dr. Bodh Raj Mainali

content.text: The words 'root canal' strike fear into many patients' hearts. Decades of misinformation have turned one of dentistry's most common and effective procedures into a source of unnecessary anxiety. At Biraj Dental in Bhaktapur, we want to set the record straight: a root canal treatment should not be painful. In fact, it relieves pain.
What Is a Root Canal?

Inside every tooth is a soft tissue called the pulp, which contains nerves, blood vessels, and connective tissue. When this pulp becomes infected or inflamed — due to deep decay, repeated dental procedures, a crack or chip, or trauma — it can cause severe pain and lead to an abscess. A root canal treatment removes the damaged pulp, cleans and disinfects the inside of the tooth, then fills and seals it. The tooth is then restored with a filling or crown.
Does It Hurt?

This is the most common question we hear. The answer: with modern local anesthesia, you should feel no pain during the procedure. Most patients describe the sensation as similar to getting a filling — pressure and vibration, but no sharp pain. The pain people associate with root canals is actually the pain of the infection itself, which the procedure resolves. After treatment, some mild soreness for a day or two is normal and easily managed with over-the-counter pain relievers.
The Step-by-Step Process at Biraj Dental

First, we take a digital X-ray to see the shape of the root canals and check for signs of infection in the surrounding bone. Then we administer local anesthesia to numb the area completely. Once you are comfortable, we place a small protective sheet called a dental dam over the tooth to keep it clean and dry during the procedure.

Next, we make a small opening in the crown of the tooth and use tiny instruments to remove the infected pulp. We carefully clean, shape, and disinfect each canal — some teeth have one canal, others have two, three, or even four. After cleaning, we fill the canals with a biocompatible material called gutta-percha and seal them with adhesive cement.

Finally, because a tooth that has had a root canal is more brittle than a healthy tooth, we usually recommend placing a crown to protect it. At our Gathaghar Chowk clinic, we can often complete the entire process in one or two visits.
How Much Does It Cost?

At Biraj Dental, root canal treatment starts from NPR 4,500, depending on which tooth needs treatment — front teeth typically have fewer canals and are simpler, while molars are more complex. We always discuss the full cost with you before beginning any treatment, with no hidden fees.
If you have been putting off a root canal out of fear, we invite you to call us at 9851031257. Our gentle, experienced team will make sure you are comfortable every step of the way.

posts.title: How to Keep Your Child's Teeth Healthy
posts.excerpt: Building good dental habits early sets the foundation for a lifetime of healthy smiles.
posts.author: Dr. Bodh Raj Mainali

content.text: A child's first dental visit sets the tone for their entire relationship with oral healthcare. At Biraj Dental's pediatric dentistry clinic in Bhaktapur, we have seen how early positive experiences create children who grow up confident about dental visits — and adults who take care of their teeth. Here is a practical guide for parents.
When Should Your Child First See a Dentist?

The Nepali Dental Association recommends a child's first dental visit by their first birthday or within six months of their first tooth appearing. This early visit is not about treatment — it is about establishing a relationship, checking that teeth are developing normally, and giving parents personalized guidance on feeding, brushing, and fluoride use.
Brushing: Start Early, Make It Fun

Even before teeth appear, gently wipe your baby's gums with a clean, damp cloth after feeding. Once the first tooth emerges, use a soft infant toothbrush with a smear of fluoride toothpaste no larger than a grain of rice. For children aged three to six, use a pea-sized amount. Brush twice daily — morning and before bed — and make it enjoyable by singing a song, using a timer, or brushing together as a family.
Foods That Help — and Harm — Young Teeth

Children's enamel is thinner and more vulnerable to acid than adult enamel. Limit sugary snacks, fruit juices, and sticky candies that cling to teeth. Instead, offer cheese, yogurt, fresh fruits, vegetables, and plenty of water. Milk is excellent for developing teeth due to its calcium content, but avoid putting a child to bed with a bottle of milk or juice — the prolonged exposure to sugars causes early childhood caries, also known as bottle rot.
Thumb Sucking and Pacifier Use

Thumb sucking and pacifier use are normal in infants and toddlers. Most children stop on their own between ages two and four. However, if the habit continues past age four, it can affect the alignment of permanent teeth and the shape of the palate. If your child is still sucking their thumb at age four or five, talk to your dentist about gentle strategies to help them stop.
Dental Sealants and Fluoride Treatments

Dental sealants are thin protective coatings applied to the chewing surfaces of back teeth, where most childhood cavities occur. They are quick, painless, and can reduce the risk of decay by up to 80%. Combined with regular fluoride treatments, sealants provide powerful protection during the years when children are most cavity-prone. Ask about these preventive options at your child's next visit to Biraj Dental in Bhaktapur.

Our pediatric dentistry services at Biraj Dental start from just NPR 600. We create a warm, child-friendly environment where your little ones feel safe and comfortable. Call 9851031257 to book your child's appointment today.

items.q: Where exactly is Biraj Dental located?
items.a: Our main branch is at Gathaghar Chowk (Namunabasti), Bhaktapur. We also have 8 additional locations across Bagmati Province: Jorpati (Kathmandu), Sallaghari (Bhaktapur), Chabahil (Kathmandu), Suryabinayak (Bhaktapur), Charikot (Dolakha), Chautara (Sindhupalchowk), and Banepa (Kavre).

Our main branch at Gathaghar Chowk is open Sunday–Friday 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Saturday 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM. Other branches may have slightly different hours. Call 9851031257 for your nearest branch hours.

items.q: Do I need an appointment, or can I walk in?
items.a: Walk-ins are welcome, but we recommend booking an appointment to minimize wait time. You can book online through our website or call us at 9851031257.

items.q: Which dental services do you offer?
items.a: We offer General Checkup & Cleaning, Tooth Extraction, Dental Fillings, Root Canal Treatment, Teeth Whitening, Dental Implants, Orthodontics/Braces, and Pediatric Dentistry across all our branches.

items.q: Is root canal treatment painful at Biraj Dental?
items.a: Not at all. We use modern anesthesia and techniques to ensure root canal treatment is virtually painless. Most patients report it feels similar to getting a filling.

items.q: How much does professional teeth whitening cost?
items.a: Professional teeth whitening starts from NPR 5,000. The exact cost depends on the level of staining and the method used. We'll discuss options during your consultation.

items.q: Do you provide dental care for children?
items.a: Yes! Our pediatric dentistry services are designed specifically for children. We create a comfortable, friendly environment that makes dental visits a positive experience for young patients.

All our branches offer general dentistry, fillings, extractions, and cleanings. Specialized services like root canals and implants are primarily handled at our main branch in Gathaghar Chowk, Bhaktapur. Visit the branch closest to you for general care.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: We accept cash and card payments at all locations. We believe in transparent pricing with no hidden fees. Payment is due at the time of service.
